files.SKILL.md:
Read `/workspace/INSTRUCTION.md` and produce the deliverable at the specified path. Do not declare done until it exists and the system behaves as specified.

## Always do

Always verify flag values via `<command> --help` (or `man <command>`) before using them.

If a service-management command is needed, check that it exists first; fall back to an alternative when absent.

## When editing hierarchical configuration files

Change config files by full overwrite (`cat > FILE << 'EOF' ... EOF`), never by in-place edit. Partial edits misplace content.

In configs with nested scopes, reusable named declarations (log/format definitions, named zones, upstream aliases, shared resources) belong at the outermost scope that contains all references to them — not at a child scope. Declarations placed at the wrong level may be silently ignored or cause the entire reload to be rejected. Use the same name everywhere a declaration is referenced.

When a service offers a drop-in include directory for fragments, prefer writing a new file there over editing the main config. Read the main file's include directive(s) first to confirm the drop-in path is actually picked up — some daemons require a specific subdirectory pattern, others require a symlink from a staging area into an enabled area. If a stock default exists at the same include path that conflicts with your fragment, remove or override it.

## When a non-root process writes to a directory

Directories a non-root process will write to must be owned by that process's user. `mkdir` leaves them owned by the user running the script — pair with `chown` when the runtime user differs.

## Before declaring done

Re-read your script and mentally trace one full execution as the lowest-privilege user that will run at runtime: for each directory write, file create, and service start, ask "does the runtime user have permission, and is the target ready?" Also check that only the include lines the task explicitly requires are present — drop unrelated defaults the task doesn't mention. Fix any gap.

Run your script; if it exits non-zero, iterate until it exits cleanly.
